JC placement / regression:
HJC via Harrington (2007) single-variable regression (eq 5–7), RIGHT hip, mm, in the ASIS-midpoint pelvic frame (x ant, y sup, z right):
  x̂ = −0.24·PD − 9.9   (antero-posterior)
  ŷ = −0.30·PW − 10.9  (supero-inferior)
  ẑ = +0.33·PW + 7.3   (medio-lateral)
LEFT hip: negate ẑ. PW = inter-ASIS width; PD = ASIS-midpoint→PSIS-midpoint distance. ORIGIN = midpoint of the two ASIS (NOT the HJC). The located HJC then becomes the origin of the Wu/Dumas pelvic JCS frame. LOOCV error ≈5 mm (AP), ≈4 mm (SI, ML). Multi-var alt for y: ŷ=−0.16·PW−0.04·LL−7.1.
e1 axis (proximal) + motion: Z (pelvic CS): ∥ line joining R & L ASIS, points right. Rotation α = flexion / extension
e2 axis (floating) + motion: Floating axis ⊥ e1 & e3. Rotation β = adduction / abduction
e3 axis (distal) + motion: y (femoral CS): FE-midpoint → HJC, points cranial. Rotation γ = internal / external rotation
Sign conventions: Right-handed frames as defined. Wu 2002 gives no explicit polarity for hip; adopt JCS standard: +flexion about +Z, +adduction, +internal rotation (right); mirror left.
Axis obliquity / non-orthogonality: Ball-and-socket; 3 rotational DOF treated as orthogonal in the JCS. No obliquity correction.
Freyja status / deviation log: Pelvic CS = Dumas pelvis SCS exactly → coherent with BSIP, no transform. HJC LOCATED via Harrington 2007 (eq 5–7); output is relative to the ASIS-midpoint, and that located point becomes the JCS/BSIP frame origin. CROSS-CHECK vs locked inter-HJC 173.2 mm: inter-HJC = 0.66·PW + 14.6 ⇒ implies inter-ASIS PW ≈ 240 mm (each HJC 86.6 mm lateral of ASIS midpoint). Verify against CAD/MakeHuman pelvic width before joint freeze. Note: Harrington needs PD & PW — soft-tissue-biased in gait labs, but you have TRUE bony values from CAD, so it is cleaner for Freyja than for its validation population.

JC placement / regression: KJC = femoral epicondyle midpoint (motion-analysis convention, matches policy). G&S original locates origins on the mechanical axes through the intercondylar points (from biplanar X-ray); for Freyja use the epicondyle-midpoint KJC to stay coherent with the Dumas thigh/shank frames.
SCS origin: KJC = femoral epicondyle midpoint. [G&S original: tibial mechanical axis where it crosses subchondral bone between the intercondylar eminences.]
e1 axis (proximal) + motion: e1 = femoral mediolateral axis (through condyles/epicondyles), fixed in femur, points right. Rotation α = flexion (+) / extension (−). [G&S label: e1 = I; reference e1ʳ = J anterior]
e2 axis (floating) + motion: e2 = floating axis = (e3 × e1)/|e3 × e1|, common perpendicular. Rotation β = abduction (+) / adduction (−). Magnitude via cos β = I·k; sign is side-dependent (R: adduction = β − π/2; L: π/2 − β).
e3 axis (distal) + motion: e3 = tibial long (mechanical) axis, fixed in tibia, points proximal. Rotation γ = external rotation (+) / internal rotation (−). [G&S label: e3 = k; reference e3ʳ = j anterior]
Rotation sequence: e1→e2→e3: flexion (femoral ML) → ab/adduction (floating) → int/ext rotation (tibial long axis). SEQUENCE-INDEPENDENT — the central G&S result: JCS angles do not depend on decomposition order (unlike Euler angles).
Sign conventions: Per G&S Fig 3 (right knee): flexion +, abduction +, external rotation +. Side-dependent flips for ab/adduction and tibial rotation (L vs R). Translations: lateral tibial shift q1 +, anterior drawer q2 +, joint distraction q3 +.
Axis obliquity / non-orthogonality: Flexion axis e1 is NOT the screw axis — the screw/helical axis is skewed (it combines tibial rotation + adduction with flexion). G&S deliberately separate the clinical flexion axis from the screw axis. e1 ⊥ e3 only at neutral; off-neutral the system is non-orthogonal, coupling translations (e.g. distraction along e3 also shifts ML). Singularity at β=0 (90° abduction) / gimbal lock — non-issue for the knee, but watch it at the shoulder.
Freyja status / deviation log: FRAMEWORK SOURCE for the whole sheet — every other joint inherits this e1/e2/e3 floating-axis JCS. Build the femoral & tibial Cartesian frames as the Dumas thigh/shank segment frames for BSIP coherence (e1 = ML/right axis, e3 = long/proximal axis). NB: G&S's 'four-link cylindrical chain' (Fig 6) is the JCS's kinematic abstraction — distinct from your anatomical crossed-four-bar ACL/PCL knee linkage; don't conflate.

JC placement / regression: Origin = IM (inter-malleolar point, midway MM–LM tips). Landmark midpoint, per ISB.
e1 axis (proximal) + motion: Z (tibia/fibula CS): line MM→LM, points right. Rotation α = dorsiflexion (+) / plantarflexion (−)
e2 axis (floating) + motion: Floating axis ⊥ e1 & e3. Rotation β = inversion (+) / eversion (−)
e3 axis (distal) + motion: y (calcaneus CS): long axis of tibia/fibula in neutral, points cranial. Rotation γ = internal (+) / external (−) rotation
Sign conventions: Dorsiflexion +, inversion +, internal rotation + (right). Translations: lateral shift +, anterior drawer +, compression +.
Axis obliquity / non-orthogonality: This is the ANKLE COMPLEX (talocrural + subtalar LUMPED) — ISB deferred separate talocrural/subtalar standards. Real talocrural & subtalar axes are oblique; matters when the ankle is split for actuation.
Freyja status / deviation log: Foot-to-shank lumped complex. For Freyja's actuated ankle, decide: model talocrural + subtalar separately (oblique axes) vs lumped single axis.

JC placement / regression: Origin = LJC (lumbar joint centre). From Reed 1999 / Dumas female appendix.
SCS origin: LJC (proximal joint centre); regional application of intervertebral JCS
e1 axis (proximal) + motion: Z (proximal segment CS): ∥ pedicle-base line, points right. Rotation α = flexion / extension
e2 axis (floating) + motion: Floating axis ⊥ e1 & e3. Rotation β = lateral bending
e3 axis (distal) + motion: y (distal segment CS): through vertebral endplate centres, points cephalad. Rotation γ = axial rotation
Rotation sequence: e1→e2→e3: Z (flex/ext) → floating (lateral bend) → y (axial rot)
Sign conventions: Right-handed as defined. Wu 2002 spine assigns no explicit polarity; adopt +flexion, +right lateral bending, +rightward axial rotation.
Axis obliquity / non-orthogonality: CRITICAL: intervertebral joints have NO fixed axis — only an instantaneous axis of rotation. 6-DOF with coupling (off-diagonal stiffness; Panjabi 1976). Helical axis is the alternative. Regional value = summation over levels.
Freyja status / deviation log: Directly relevant to continuum-spine work: ISB itself treats spine motion as no-fixed-axis + coupled DOF. If the spine participates in balance, this coupling must be present in the MuJoCo model.

e3 axis (distal) + motion: y (distal segment CS): through vertebral endplate centres, points cephalad. Rotation γ = axial rotation
